title: Colymbada carolipauana
taxonomy: Colymbada carolipauana is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family. It belongs to the genus Colymbada and is native to Brazil. Colymbada carolipauana is closely related to other species within the genus, which are often found in rocky areas and open woodlands. The classification of Colymbada carolipauana is further supported by its morphology and genetic characteristics, which are unique to this species. This classification is essential for understanding the evolutionary history and relationships between Colymbada carolipauana and other related species.

benefits: Colymbada carolipauana, also known as Brazilian giant marigold, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits. constituents: Colymbada carolipauana, also known as Brazilian chamomile, contains active constituents that contribute to its medicinal properties. These constituents primarily include flavonoids, sesquiterpene lactones, and terpenoids. The plant's flavonoids, such as apigenin and luteolin, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
